title: Secondary workflow path
p3_observations: P3: secondary_workflow_receipt_fields is a required-subset list rather than an exhaustive receipt key list; all 13 emitted receipt keys are nonetheless validated by assertControlPlaneClaudeReviewSecondaryWorkflowPathResult, matching the established S07 convention.

P3: fail_closed_on labels are declarative metadata rather than literal claim keys; enforcement happens through forbiddenSecondaryWorkflowClaims plus unknown-claim rejection, matching the established S07 convention.
review_text: Claude returned GO for RP00.P02.M04.S08 with no P0, P1, or P2 findings. Claude verified that precheckControlPlaneClaudeReviewSecondaryWorkflowPath consumes and revalidates the S07 result, preserves claude_review plus claude-opus-4-8 plus max plus read-only metadata, emits a frozen metadata-only secondary workflow receipt, rejects forbidden and unknown claims, and does not invoke runtime Claude, write review queues, write audit ledgers/events, execute runtime permission/AuthZ/permission-engine logic, execute service logic, create runtime routes, or write product state. Two P3 observations were informational only: receipt field advertising is a required subset, and fail_closed_on labels are declarative metadata backed by explicit forbidden-claim enforcement.

findings.title: secondary_workflow_receipt_fields advertises 10 fields while the emitted secondary_workflow_receipt object carries 13 keys.
findings.body: No action required. The list is a minimum-required subset, and assertControlPlaneClaudeReviewSecondaryWorkflowPathResult explicitly checks all emitted receipt keys.

findings.title: fail_closed_on labels are declarative metadata rather than literal claim keys.
findings.body: No action required. Actual enforcement is performed by forbiddenSecondaryWorkflowClaims plus unknown-claim rejection, consistent with the S07 pattern.
